alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years at Google scaling global commercial operations; experience in requirements definition, stakeholder engagement, and translating business needs into operational solutions
alignments.job_requirement: Collaborate with customers to uncover business challenges and determine how solutions can help achieve business objectives

alignments.candidate_evidence: Led transformation programs at Google with cross-functional ownership; demonstrated ability to influence senior stakeholders and manage complex operational change
alignments.job_requirement: Drive change management and value realization; build strong relationships with customers as a trusted advisor

alignments.candidate_evidence: Background in scaling operations and transformation at high-growth company; experience designing processes and solutions, though primarily business/operational rather than AI/technical
alignments.job_requirement: Work in fast-paced environment, think analytically and creatively, apply technology solutions to business problems

gaps.gap: Role requires bachelors/masters in Engineering, Computer Science, Data Science, AI/ML, or Mathematics; candidate background is operations/strategy without disclosed formal degree in these fields
gaps.mitigation: Candidate has demonstrated analytical and operational learning capability; could rapidly develop domain knowledge through Celonis enablement program, but lacks foundational technical credentialing the role explicitly values

gaps.gap: Role requires hands-on SQL and/or Python experience; candidate has only basic Python and is actively learning
gaps.mitigation: Graduate program includes training, but entering with below-baseline technical competency for a role focused on prototyping and developing AI solutions

gaps.gap: Role emphasizes prototyping, developing AI/ML and LLM-based solutions; candidate's experience is in business operations and transformation, not solution design or technical implementation
gaps.mitigation: Orbit program is structured for enablement, but this is a fundamentally different skill axis from candidate's core background

gaps.gap: Role requires fluency in Dutch and English; candidate is fluent in English and German but Dutch not mentioned
gaps.mitigation: Dutch can be acquired, but is an explicit requirement for customer-facing Benelux engagements

gaps.gap: Associate/entry-level scope misaligned with candidate's eight-year senior experience and demonstrated L4-equivalent ownership; overqualified by seniority but underqualified by technical domain
gaps.mitigation: None—this is a structural mismatch. Candidate should target mid-level VE or business-focused roles, not a graduate program

hard_blockers.evidence: JD states: 'You have a bachelors and/or masters degree in (Industrial) Engineering, Computer Science, Data Science / Business Analytics, AI/ML, Mathematics or other related fields.' Candidate profile does not disclose a degree in any of these fields; background is described as strategy and operations at Google.

JD states: 'An effective communicator and fluent in Dutch and English.' Candidate is fluent in English and German but Dutch proficiency is not mentioned in the profile. Customer-facing Benelux role explicitly requires Dutch.

output.summary: This is a junior/graduate-level technical engineer role requiring a CS/Engineering/Data Science degree, SQL/Python proficiency, and Dutch language fluency. The candidate is a senior strategy and operations leader (L4-equivalent) with eight years at Google in transformation and commercial scaling—fundamentally misaligned by both domain (AI/technical prototyping vs. business operations) and seniority (over-qualified for Orbit Program but under-qualified technically). Hard blockers: no disclosed technical degree and no Dutch fluency. Skip this role and pursue mid-level Value Engineering, Product Operations, or business-facing strategy roles.
